name: Tacos-Mexican Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Divide the uncooked ground beef into twelve equal portions using a table knife.
2. Spread each portion on half of each tortilla, leaving at least 1/2-inch from the center to make folding easier.
3. Add salt and pepper or seasoned salt as desired.
4. Heat 1/2-inch of oil in a ten-inch skillet until water droplets placed in the oil pop vigorously.
5. Gently place one tortilla into the skillet, meat side up, towards the outside. Place another tortilla on the opposite side of the skillet.
6. The portions of the tortillas without ground beef will overlap. Use metal salad tongs to gently press these parts into the hot oil until they become soft.
7. Fold the half with the meat over the half with no meat using the salad tongs. Fry for 2-3 minutes on each side for "soft" tacos, or longer if you prefer them crispier (do not overcook).
8. Once done, grasp both tacos near the fold and turn them upside down to drain excess oil. Gently shake the oil back into the skillet.
9. Place cooked tacos on plenty of paper towels to absorb any remaining grease.
10. Serve tacos filled with shredded lettuce, grated cheese, tomato, refried beans, onion, olives, sour cream, avocado, taco sauce, and other desired toppings.
